competitors.name: Test My Reaction Time
features: Results can be shared on social media
weaknesses: No account system to save results

competitors.name: Reaction Time Test by Math is Fun
features: Part of a larger educational site
weaknesses: No user accounts or saved results
Limited to one type of test

competitors.name: Reaction Time Test by Exploratorium
features: Part of a science museum's website
weaknesses: Limited to one type of test
No user accounts or saved results
Not as user-friendly as other sites

competitors.name: Reaction Time Test by CogniFit
weaknesses: Pricing can be a barrier for some users

gaps.implementation: Develop a dedicated mobile app for iOS and Android with the same features as the web version, plus additional mobile-specific features like push notifications.
Introduce a dashboard where users can track their progress over time, compare their results with others, and receive personalized insights and recommendations.
Allow users to customize various aspects of the test, such as the color of the stimulus, the duration of the test, and the type of feedback provided.

gaps.gap: Integration with health and fitness apps
gaps.implementation: Enable integration with popular health and fitness apps (e.g., Apple Health, Google Fit) to provide a more holistic view of the user's overall performance and well-being.
Add gamification elements such as badges, levels, and challenges to make the experience more engaging and motivating for users.

recommended_positioning: A comprehensive, user-friendly, and scientifically validated reaction time testing platform with advanced analytics and mobile app support.
